seo.title: Denis Kukobin — QA Lead
seo.description: QA Lead portfolio rendered as a bug ticket: process, Playwright automation, observability, and testing high-load microservices.

title.name: Denis Kukobin
title.headline: QA Lead — turning release chaos into predictable quality.

description.body: Senior QA Engineer with 6+ years in high-load products: iGaming, fintech, blockchain, LLM platforms. Frontend developer background (React, Next.js) — I understand architecture and testability from the inside. QA Lead at SprutGaming: 21 microservices, Go / Kafka / K8s. I introduced AI-assisted QA as a company standard and built the QA function from scratch with a team of 14.

items: Started as a frontend developer (React, Next.js) — I understand code from the inside and test with source access.
4 years at Aurora Infinity: blockchain DEX, crypto platforms, LLM chat — testing products more complex than average.
Moved to iGaming: 21 microservices, Go / Kafka / Kubernetes — high-load B2B platform.
Introduced AI-assisted QA as a company standard: LLM-based DB-migration verification, pixel-perfect, Cursor IDE + Figma MCP.
QA Lead with a 14-person team: processes from scratch, shift-left, DoD, C-level reporting.

items.detail: First QA role: white-box testing of a custom CRM, manual and integration testing, frontend in React in parallel. Became the sole QA by the end.
4 years across multiple products: author social network, L2 ETH DEX, crypto platform. TypeScript, React, Playwright, Jest (~70% coverage), mobile testing via TestFlight.
Most complex product — LLM chat: streaming AI responses, MCP servers, agent tool calls, crypto subscriptions. Testing what isn't in any textbook yet.
iGaming B2B platform: white-label sites, admin panel, payment flows (crypto invoice, H2H, fiat), PHP→Go migrations. Introduced AI-assisted QA as a team workflow.
Team of 14 (9 core + 5 contractors). Built QA from zero, shift-left, two-cycle testing, onboarded 6 engineers, syncs with CTO 3× per week.

items: Scale AI-assisted QA: from point tools to a systemic team-wide approach.
Build a mature shift-left culture — quality designed in at the requirements stage, not caught in production.
Grow a team of people who reason in risks and architecture, not just test cases.
Reduce escaped defects and MTTR through automation and observability.
Give back: standards, articles, talks — move the QA community forward.

items.title: AI-assisted QA as a company standard
items.sub: LLM-based DB migration verification, pixel-perfect via AI, e2e with Cursor IDE + Figma MCP. Formalized in the internal QA regulation.

items.title: QA from zero: shift-left + two-cycle testing
items.sub: No docs, no test cases on day one. Built checklists → test cases → AC review → smoke + e2e + DoD. Adopted as a company-wide process standard.

items.title: Team of 14 from scratch
items.sub: Onboarded 6 engineers (all still with the company), led 9 core QA + 5 contractors, daily prioritization and C-level reporting.

items.title: LLM chat: testing AI agents
items.sub: Streaming responses, MCP servers, agent tool calls, chat async behavior, crypto subscriptions — full QA cycle on a non-standard product.

attachments.intro: Attaching what usually ships as files: recent QA writing and direct contacts.

articles.indexDescription: Notes on QA, automation, and observability — no filler, no bureaucracy.
